info.name: Gear-up
info.description:
# Gear-up API

**Gear-up** is a gear rental platform API that enables customers to browse and rent equipment, providers to list and manage their gear inventory, and administrators to oversee the entire platform.

---

## User Roles

| Role | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Customer** | Browses available gear, places rental orders, makes payments, and leaves reviews |
| **Provider** | Lists gear for rent, manages inventory, and fulfills rental orders |
| **Admin** | Manages users, categories, and platform-wide operations |

---

## API Modules

| Module | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **auth** | User registration, login, token refresh, and logout |
| **user** | User profile management and account settings |
| **category** | Gear categories — create, read, update, and delete |
| **gear** | Gear listings — browse, search, and manage rental items |
| **rental_order** | Create and manage rental orders and their lifecycle |
| **payment** | Payment processing and transaction history |
| **admin** | Admin-only operations for platform management |
| **reviews** | Submit and retrieve reviews for rented gear |

---

## Authentication

Most endpoints require a **Bearer token** in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer <your_token>

 ```

Obtain a token by logging in via the **auth** module. Tokens should be stored and reused across requests.

However Tokens will be auto saved in your cookies. So you don't have to send a Authorization header

---

## Base URL

Configure your environment with one of the following variables:

- `{{backend_base}}` — Production / staging base URL
    
- `{{backend_dev}}` — Local development base URL
    
- Use {{backend_base}} = [https://gearup-backend-soqv.onrender.com](https://gearup-backend-soqv.onrender.com) or [https://gearup-backend-gold.vercel.app/](https://gearup-backend-gold.vercel.app/) in vercel. Both works fine. I recommend to try with vercel initially.
    

Set the appropriate variable in the active Gear-up environment before sending requests.

item.name: auth

request.description:
## Register User

Registers a new user account on the Gear-up platform. The request body must include the user's name, email, password, and role (`customer` or `provider`). Upon successful registration, the server creates the account and may return a JWT token or a confirmation message. This endpoint is publicly accessible and does not require authentication.

## Login User (Admin)

The returned token must be stored and passed in the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header for all admin-protected routes. Use this request to test admin-specific workflows.

item.description:
## Authentication

This folder contains all endpoints related to user authentication on the Gear-up platform. It handles new user registration and login for all three user roles: **customer**, **provider**, and **admin**. A valid JWT token returned from login must be included in the `Authorization` header for all protected routes across the API.

item.name: user

request.description:
## Get Profile

Retrieves the profile information of the currently authenticated user. The response includes details such as name, email, role, and any additional profile fields. This endpoint is accessible to all authenticated users (customer, provider, or admin) and requires a valid JWT token in the `Authorization` header.

## Update User

Updates the profile information of the currently authenticated user. The request body can include fields such as name, phone number, address, or profile picture. Only the fields provided in the request body will be updated (partial update). This endpoint is accessible to all authenticated users and requires a valid JWT token in the `Authorization` header.

request.description:
## Delete User

Deletes the account of the currently authenticated user from the Gear-up platform. This is a permanent action and cannot be undone — all associated data may also be removed. This endpoint requires a valid JWT token in the `Authorization` header and can be used by any authenticated user to delete their own account.

item.description:
## User

This folder covers user profile management for authenticated users on the Gear-up platform. It includes endpoints to retrieve, update, and delete a user's own profile. All requests in this folder require a valid JWT token in the `Authorization` header.

item.name: category

request.description:
## Create Category

Creates a new gear category on the Gear-up platform (e.g., Fitness, Camping, Cycling). The request body must include the category name and optionally a description. This endpoint is restricted to **admin** users only and requires a valid admin JWT token in the `Authorization` header. Categories created here can be assigned to gear listings.

response.name: CREATE_CATEGORY

request.description:
## Get All Categories

Retrieves a list of all available gear categories on the Gear-up platform. This endpoint is publicly accessible and does not require authentication. The response includes each category's ID, name, and description, which can be used to filter gear listings by category.

response.name: GET_ALL_CATEGORY

request.description:
## Delete Category (Admin)

Permanently deletes a gear category by its ID. Replace the `{id}` path variable with the ID of the category to be deleted. This endpoint is restricted to **admin** users only and requires a valid admin JWT token in the `Authorization` header. Note that deleting a category may affect gear listings that are currently assigned to it.

response.name: DELETE_CATEGORY_ADMIN

request.description:
### **Update Category By Admin**

This is the section where Admin can update their created category, They can either update the name of the category or they can update the description of the category. Any other person rather than an Admin is prohibited in this route.

response.name: Update_Category

item.description:
## Category

This folder manages gear categories on the Gear-up platform (e.g., Fitness, Camping, Water Sports). Categories are used to organize and filter gear listings. Creating and deleting categories is restricted to **admin** users, while fetching categories is publicly accessible.

## Create New Gear

Creates a new gear listing on the Gear-up platform. The request body should include details such as gear name, description, category, rental price per day, availability, and images. This endpoint is restricted to **provider** users and requires a valid provider JWT token in the `Authorization` header. The created gear will be associated with the authenticated provider's account.

item.description:
## Gear

This folder contains all endpoints for managing gear listings on the Gear-up platform. Providers can create, update, and delete their own gear items, while customers and guests can browse and search available gear. Gear items belong to a category and include details such as pricing, availability, and description.

item.name: rental_order

request.description:
## Create Rental Order

Creates a new rental order for a specific gear item. The request body should include the gear ID, rental start date, and rental end date. This endpoint is restricted to **customer** users and requires a valid customer JWT token in the `Authorization` header. Upon creation, the order will be in a pending state awaiting approval from the provider.

request.description:
## See My Rentals (Customer)

Retrieves all rental orders placed by the currently authenticated customer. The response includes order details such as gear info, rental dates, status (pending, approved, cancelled, returned), and payment status. This endpoint is restricted to **customer** users and requires a valid customer JWT token in the `Authorization` header.

request.description:
## See My Rentals (Provider)

Retrieves all rental orders associated with the gear listings owned by the currently authenticated provider. The response includes order details such as customer info, gear info, rental dates, and current order status. This endpoint is restricted to **provider** users and requires a valid provider JWT token in the `Authorization` header.

request.description:
## Update Order Status (Provider)

Allows a provider to update the status of a rental order (e.g., approve, reject, or mark as returned). Replace `{id}` in the URL with the rental order's ID. The request body should include the new status value. This endpoint is restricted to **provider** users and requires a valid provider JWT token in the `Authorization` header.

request.description:
## Cancel Order (Customer)

Allows a customer to cancel an existing rental order by its ID. Replace `{id}` in the URL with the rental order's ID. Cancellation may only be possible if the order is still in a pending or approved state — orders that have already been fulfilled may not be cancellable. This endpoint is restricted to **customer** users and requires a valid customer JWT token in the `Authorization` header.

item.description:
## Rental Order

This folder handles the full rental order lifecycle on the Gear-up platform. Customers can create rental orders and cancel them, while providers can view incoming orders and update their status (e.g., approve, reject, mark as returned). All endpoints require authentication with the appropriate user role.

item.name: payment

request.description:
## Create Checkout Session

Initiates a Stripe checkout session for a rental order payment. The request body should include the rental order ID and any other required payment details. Upon success, the server returns a Stripe session URL that the customer can be redirected to for completing the payment. This endpoint is restricted to **customer** users and requires a valid customer JWT token in the `Authorization` header.

response.name: CREATE_CHECKOUT_SESSION

request.description:

This endpoint is restricted to **customer** users and requires a valid customer JWT token in the `Authorization` header.

item.description:
## Admin

This folder contains administrative endpoints for managing the Gear-up platform. Admins can view all users, gear listings, and rental orders, as well as suspend or reactivate user accounts. All endpoints in this folder are restricted to users with the **admin** role and require a valid admin JWT token.

request.description:
## Create Review

Submits a review for a gear item that the customer has rented. The request body should include the gear ID, a rating (e.g., 1–5 stars), and an optional comment. Reviews can only be submitted by **customers** who have completed a rental for the specified gear. This endpoint requires a valid customer JWT token in the `Authorization` header.

request.description:
## View Reviews of a Gear

Retrieves all reviews submitted for a specific gear item. The response includes each review's rating, comment, reviewer name, and timestamp. This endpoint is publicly accessible and does not require authentication, allowing potential customers to read reviews before deciding to rent a gear item. Pass the gear's ID as a path variable in the URL.
